name: Twenty
one_liner: Twenty is an open-source, highly customizable CRM platform designed for modern teams, offering flexible data models, workflow automation, and extensive API capabilities for managing customer relationships.
auth_notes: API keys are generated from the Twenty app's API settings page and are used as secret tokens in the request header. Some examples show the API key being used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
access_notes: Twenty is open-source and can be self-hosted, allowing developers to get working API credentials for free. For the cloud version, a free trial is available, and API access is included.
api_notes: Twenty offers both REST and GraphQL APIs, including a Core API for CRUD operations on records (people, companies, opportunities, custom objects) and a Metadata API for schema management (create/modify/delete objects, fields, relations). The API is generated from the workspace schema, meaning custom objects and fields automatically get API endpoints, indicating broad control over the product. A `twenty-sdk` is also available for application development.
mcp_notes: Multiple community-driven Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers exist for Twenty CRM, enabling integration with AI assistants like Claude. These projects expose Twenty's GraphQL and REST APIs as MCP tools.

evidence.claim: Twenty is an open-source CRM that provides a flexible, customizable platform for managing customer relationships.
The API uses API keys for authentication, which are generated from the app's settings and included in the request header.
Twenty offers both REST and GraphQL APIs, covering core CRM data and metadata for schema management.
The API is generated from the workspace schema, meaning custom objects and fields automatically get API endpoints.
Community-driven Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers exist for Twenty CRM, enabling AI assistant integration.
